title: AI, Design, and the Power of Open Models
content: Ideogram CEO Mohammad Norouzi discusses image generation models and design workflows with Yoko Li and Justine Moore. He covers Ideogram's open-weight model release, challenges in generating text and layouts, and the growing importance of controllability in AI research. The conversation addresses prompting, customization, editing, and tradeoffs between general-purpose and specialized creative models. Norouzi also shares perspectives on open-source AI, design tools, agentic workflows, and future evolution of image generation for creators and enterprises seeking greater output control.

title: 裁，都裁，大的要来了对吗
content: A Chinese tech worker recounts layoff experiences in the Web3/crypto industry since 2018, including multiple rounds due to funding crises and market cycles. The author describes harsh interview questions about their volatile background and details mass layoffs at a major exchange despite management promises. The company allegedly planned terminations a year in advance, used anonymous peer voting to identify targets, and scapegoated employees. The author predicts further industry turmoil ahead.

title: -270℃的太空居然是“散热地狱”？SpaceX偏要把服务器扔上去！ | 原文
content: SpaceX plans to deploy servers in space despite extreme cooling challenges. While space nears absolute zero (-270°C), the vacuum lacks air or water to conduct heat away, making thermal dissipation extremely difficult. Without physical media for heat transfer, satellites can only rely on radiation cooling through blackbody radiation.

title: Fable 5突遭封禁，Anthropic大会差点黄了
content: Anthropic's June 13 Claude Fable 5 developer event in San Francisco was disrupted when the AI model was suddenly banned. The company substituted Opus 4.8, and developers still created impressive demos including real-time room translation, 3D historical building reconstruction, and instant 3D furniture rendering. Before the ban, Fable 5 had produced notable projects: a "faster-than-light museum" simulation, 3D Hogwarts from a single prompt, reverse-engineering a 1993 DOS game into an iPhone app in 30 minutes, self-verifying parkour physics, and a procedurally generated universe from three prompts. Anthropic's technical staff flew to Washington to address the issue. The ban marked the first time an AI model was suspended for being "too capable."

title: 头一回，我被工信部的新车干沉默了
content: A review of recent MIIT vehicle filings highlights several polarizing designs. The Huawei-backed Luxeed RX targets the Xiaomi YU7 with sporty proportions and 437kW dual motors. Stelato's G9 flagship features speaker-inspired lighting and an awkward backup camera placement. BAIC's Beijing 81 VJ merges styling cues from Mercedes, Land Rover, and Ineos. Audi's new Q3L adopts divisive split headlights but gains 15cm in length. BYD's Denza Z offers a 1,180kW tri-motor convertible supercar, while the 5.2-meter "Da Han" sedan prioritizes luxury over raw performance. The author notes that as hardware converges, design differentiation increasingly reveals corporate priorities.

title: 21 years and counting of 'eight fallacies of distributed computing' (2025)
content: Peter Deutsch's "Eight Fallacies of Distributed Computing" remain relevant after 21 years. The fallacies—assuming reliable networks, zero latency, infinite bandwidth, secure systems, homogeneous infrastructure, stable topology, single administrator, and zero transport cost—continue to mislead developers. Despite advances in cloud computing and distributed systems, engineers still fall prey to these assumptions, leading to system failures and poor design decisions.

title: 硬氪专访 | 智源研究院院长王仲远：VLA不会死，但世界模型是未来
content: Wang Zhongyuan, president of Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence (BAAI), discusses world models as the next-generation foundation model for AI to transition from digital to physical worlds. He identifies four current approaches—language-centered, pixel-centered, 3D structure-centered, and visual representation-centered—while BAAI pursues a fifth path: unified latent space representation compressing all modalities. World models must achieve physical correctness, causal reasoning, long-term consistency, and generalization to serve as true "robot brains." He compares the field's current stage to deep learning in 2012, estimating 3-5 years until breakthrough. Critical challenges include scarce real-world physical data and unresolved data mixing ratios. While VLA models suffice for narrow tasks today, world models represent the future for generalizable embodied intelligence requiring complex reasoning and active exploration.

title: 想“躺着瘦”的年轻人，买爆减肥药
content: Young Chinese are buying weight-loss drugs in record numbers, driven by social media-fueled body anxiety and the promise of effortless weight loss. GLP-1 drugs like tirzepatide and semaglutide topped global pharmaceutical sales in 2025, with China's e-commerce sales surging 2,414% year-over-year. While users celebrate rapid results, many experience severe side effects including nausea, depression, facial aging, and weight rebound after stopping. Unlike the US market targeting obesity-related health issues, China's marketing increasingly targets already-thin users seeking "perfect" bodies through disguised social media ads, as drug prices plunge amid fierce competition.

Morgan Stanley's recent closed-door meeting analyzed the recent tech pullback and latest developments in memory, MLCC, and CPO supply chains. The conclusion: the recent correction is a healthy reset, with AI infrastructure driving structural upcycles in memory and passive components.

Memory is shifting from cyclical to AI-driven structural growth. The pullback stems from profit-taking and ETF deleveraging, not cycle end. Unlike past consumer electronics-driven cycles, AI infrastructure now drives demand. Lower memory costs in AI scenarios stimulate deployment demand rather than squeezing profits. Supply faces constraints from cleanroom limits, EUV equipment, and HBM capacity conversion physical bottlenecks. Q3 DRAM contract prices expected to rise 20-30%. HBM profitability currently below server DRAM; suppliers aim to raise HBM prices through LTAs (projected +50-100% by 2027). LTAs enhance 3-5 year earnings visibility, justifying 10x PE

Insta360's revenue surged 83% year-over-year in Q1 2026, but its net profit margin collapsed to just 3.4% due to price wars, raw material costs, and soaring expenses—sales costs doubled to ¥1.68 billion, while R&D spending nearly doubled to ¥1.53 billion and was fully expensed. Life and investing follow probability principles: single outcomes are samples, not true skill. Results stem from ability, choices, environment, and randomness. Effort improves success odds and stability. Update beliefs with new evidence via Bayes' theorem. Avoid survivor bias and gambling on luck. Repeated trials filter noise, revealing genuine competence—this underpins long-term thinking. However, repeatedly taking small-probability high-risk actions makes disaster likely over time.
